mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of co-occurring periods, or the interaction term loses explanatory power with F-stat p > 0.05 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening economic output and capacity utilization, which boosts near-term corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price indices combined with restrictive federal funds rates create a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins and compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and high-rate regime produces positive forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a resilient labor market and sustained wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns with Rank IC < 0.01, or positive payroll surprises coincide with negative equity returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate dropping below 1.5% signals severe economic deceleration and heightened recession probability, prompting portfolio de-risking that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansions in industrial production and total employment confirm synchronized supply-side recovery and labor market tightness, reinforcing near-term earnings momentum and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >55% of synchronized periods, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.
